Package de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.label

Class ParameterlessTermLabel

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.label.ParameterlessTermLabel
All Implemented Interfaces:
TermLabel, Named, SyntaxElement, TerminalSyntaxElement
public final class ParameterlessTermLabel extends Object implements TermLabel
The Class ParameterlessTermLabel can be used to define labels without parameters. You can use a SingletonLabelFactory to create a factory for an instance of this class.
See Also:
  • SingletonLabelFactory

    Description
    static final TermLabel

    static final TermLabel

    • UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL_NAME

      public static final Name UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL_NAME
      Name of UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL.

    • UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL

      public static final TermLabel UNDEFINED_VALUE_LABEL
      Label attached to a term which denotes an undefined value. At present it is only used for the else-part of the IfExThenElse operator, when it is used for the translation of JML's \min and \max operator. It is necessary to evaluate this constant expression to be not well-defined.

    • SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL_NAME

      public static final Name SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL_NAME
      Name of SELF_COMPOSITION_LABEL.

  • Constructor Details

    • ParameterlessTermLabel

      public ParameterlessTermLabel(Name name)
      Instantiates a new simple term label.
      Parameters:
      name - the name, not null The fixed associated instantiator, may be null.

  • Method Details

    • name

      public Name name()
      Description copied from interface: Named
      returns the name of this element
      Specified by:
      name in interface Named
      Returns:
      the name of the element

    • getTLChild

      public Object getTLChild(int i)
      Retrieves the i-th parameter object of this term label.

      A term label may have structure, i.e. can be parameterized.

      Simple term labels have no parameters. This always throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException.

      Specified by:
      getTLChild in interface TermLabel
      Parameters:
      i - the number of the parameter to retrieve ( 0 <= i < getChildCount())
      Returns:
      the selected parameter

    • getTLChildCount

      public int getTLChildCount()
      Gets the number of parameters of this term label.

      Simple term labels have no parameters. This always returns 0.

      Specified by:
      getTLChildCount in interface TermLabel
      Returns:
      the number of parameters (a non-negative number)
